description: Collection of resource monitoring tools: iostat, isag, mpstat, pidstat, sadf, sar.

name: Tarsnap
description: Secure, efficient proprietary online backup service.

name: Task Manager
description: GTK3 based process manager with basic system resource monitor for Xfce.

name: Telegraf
description: Agent written in Go for collecting, processing, aggregating, and writing metrics.

name: Terminator
description: Terminal emulator supporting multiple resizable terminal panels.

name: Terminology
description: Terminal emulator by the Enlightenment project team with innovative features: file thumbnails and media play like a media player.

name: Text-Diff
description: Perl library that perform diffs on files and record sets.

name: Thonny
description: Python IDE for beginners.

name: Thunar
description: File manager that can be run as a daemon with excellent start-up and directory load times.

name: Thunar Bulk Rename
description: Change the name of multiple files at once using some criterion that applies to at least one of the files. Run with thunar -B.

name: Tilda
description: Configurable drop down terminal emulator.

name: Tiled
description: General purpose 2D level editor with powerful tile map editing features. It is built to be easy to use and is suitable for many type of games.

name: Timeshift
description: A system restore tool for Linux.

name: tmate
description: A fork of tmux aiming to help with remote access and pair programming.

name: tmux
description: BSD licensed terminal multiplexer.

name: Typography
description: Tool for working with the GNOME typography design guidelines.

name: ugrep
description: Grep alternative with a hex dump feature. ug --hexdump.

name: UKUI System Monitor
description: System monitor for UKUI.

name: Umbrello
description: Unified Modelling Language (UML) diagram program based on KDE Technology. Part of kde-sdk.

name: Unison
description: File synchronization tool that allows two replicas of a collection of files and directories to be stored on different hosts (or different disks on the same host), modified separately, and then brought up to date by propagating the changes in each replica to the other.

name: Unity
description: Unity is a flexible and powerful development platform for creating multi-platform 3D and 2D games. Proprietary, but a free version is available for certain use-cases (see here for more details).

name: Usage
description: View information about use of system resources, like memory and disk space.

name: Usbimager
description: Minimal GUI application to write compressed disk images to USB drives.

name: USBView
description: Display the topology of devices on the USB bus.

name: Valgrind
description: Tool to help find memory-management problems in programs.

name: vidir
description: Rename and delete files and directories using your editor.

name: Vifm
description: Ncurses-based two-panel file manager with vi-like keybindings.

name: Visual Studio Code
description: Visual Studio Code is a cross-platform, free and open-source text editor developed by Microsoft, written in JavaScript and TypeScript. It supports a variety of languages thanks to its extensions.

name: Vorta
description: Encrypted, deduplicated and compressed backups using Borg as backend.

name: VSCodium
description: Free/Libre Open Source Software Binaries of VSCode.

name: Webfont Bundler
description: Utility to generate WOFF, WOFF2 and the necessary CSS boilerplate from non-web font formats (OTF & TTF).

name: WezTerm
description: GPU-accelerated cross-platform terminal emulator and multiplexer implemented in Rust.

name: WoeUSB
description: Simple tool to create USB stick windows installer from an ISO image or a real DVD. (Fork of WinUSB).

name: Xarchiver
description: Lightweight desktop independent archive manager built with GTK.

name: Xfce Terminal
description: Terminal emulator included in the Xfce desktop with support for a colorized prompt and a tabbed interface.

name: Xfe
description: Microsoft Explorer-like file manager for X (X File Explorer).

name: Xmake
description: A cross-platform build utility based on Lua with a built-in package manager.

name: xplr
description: A hackable, minimal, fast TUI file explorer.

name: xterm
description: Simple terminal emulator for the X Window System. It provides DEC VT102 and Tektronix 4014 compatible terminals for programs that cannot use the window system directly.

name: xvkbd
description: Virtual keyboard for X window system.

name: xxd
description: Basic hex dump from vim.

name: xxkb
description: A lightweight keyboard layout indicator and switcher.

name: yad (Yet Another Dialog)
description: Zenity fork with more dialog types and support for custom buttons.

name: Yakuake
description: Drop-down terminal (Quake style) emulator based on Konsole. Part of kde-utilities.

name: yazi
description: Blazing fast terminal file manager written in Rust, based on async I/O.

name: Zeal
description: Offline API documentation browser for software developers.

name: Zeitgeist
description: Event aggregation framework for the user's activities and notifications (files opened, websites visited, conversations had, etc.), has several third-party front-ends.

name: zellij
description: Terminal multiplexer on steroids written in Rust

name: Zenity
description: GTK-based GUI prompts by GNOME.

name: zps
description: A small utility for listing and reaping zombie processes on GNU/Linux.

name: zutty
description: A high-end terminal for low-end systems.
